$doc: Fusion deliberation config for relentless-inception gates (Grok Build edition, v0.3.0-grok).
USER COPY lives at ~/.claude/relentless-inception-grok/fusion.config.json — edit THAT, not this shipped default (skill upgrades overwrite this file).
Secrets (provider API keys) go in ~/.claude/relentless-inception-grok/secrets.env — see assets/secrets.env.example. Never put keys in this file.

transports: 'grok' = native Grok Build sub-agent (host session, no key needed) · 'claude-cli' = headless `claude -p` (Claude subscription login, no key) · 'codex' = codex CLI (ChatGPT subscription auth, no key) · 'xai' / 'openai' / 'anthropic' / 'openrouter' = provider-direct HTTP, each enabled only when its key is in secrets.env (XAI_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENROUTER_API_KEY).
backends.* turn each transport on or off; any subset may be enabled at once and seats mix freely.

Panel design guidance (TrustedRouter DRACO artifact): the FUSER is the lever (~18-pt swing) — keep it the strongest model; the judge barely matters (cheap is fine, default honors user preference of fable-5); diversity comes from distinct models/personas, NOT temperature.

xAI facts verified live 2026-07-18 against a real key: GET api.x.ai/v1/models -> 200 (slugs incl. grok-4.5, grok-4.3, grok-4.20-0309-reasoning, grok-4.20-0309-non-reasoning, grok-4.20-multi-agent-0309, grok-build-0.1); POST /v1/chat/completions is OpenAI-compatible; grok-4.5 ACCEPTS reasoning_effort 'xhigh' (reasoning_tokens reported, prompt caching active).
$panel_notes: Default = 1 grok-4.5 (the panel expert seat, verified live) + 1 gpt-5.6-sol (xhigh) + 1 fable-5 (xhigh) — three vendors; raise any count to 2 for heavier gates.
luna/terra are deliberately NOT in the default panel (weaker than sol) but remain valid codex models if listed here.
codex model aliases the skill resolves: sol→gpt-5.6-sol, luna→gpt-5.6-luna, terra→gpt-5.6-terra; any catalog slug also accepted, ANY effort minimal..xhigh (ultra = raw CLI only).
xai seats: grok-4.5 is the default panel-expert slug; the other verified slugs above are valid config values.
$judge_note: The judge barely matters — cheap is fine. If the claude CLI is absent, fall back to a cheap grok seat (transport 'grok', low effort); judge provenance is recorded in the verdict _meta.
$fuser_note: The fuser is the lever (~18-pt swing) — default is fable-5 @xhigh via headless claude CLI, the strongest available seat. Sanctioned fallback 'grok-session' = the Grok Build session model runs the fuser as a fresh instance when the claude CLI is absent (must differ from the judge instance and from the reviewed artifact's author). Fuser provenance is always recorded in the verdict _meta.

openrouter_direct.$doc: Legacy key name — applies to ANY seat on a provider-direct HTTP transport (xai/openai/anthropic/openrouter), and only to non-reasoning calls: reasoning-mode calls manage sampling internally. Subscription transports (grok/claude-cli/codex) never take a temperature. OpenRouter fusion semantics: plugin temperature reaches the PANEL only; the judge is pinned to temp 0; plugin reasoning reaches panel+judge.
temp_warnings: Reasoning models generally do NOT take kindly to temperature overrides — leave temp unset/1.0 for: OpenAI o-series and gpt-5.x in reasoning mode (temperature is ignored or rejected with a 400), Anthropic Claude with extended thinking (requires temperature=1; setting anything else errors), DeepSeek-R1-class and Gemini thinking modes (sampling is managed internally).
Do not use temperature as a diversity mechanism — empirically it does not decorrelate panel errors; vary models/personas/context bundles instead.
If you set panel temp above 1.2 expect degraded instruction-following on most models; below 0.3 panels converge and the fusion gain collapses.
